title: Rate Limiting and Redis SLA Flow
interaction_flow: RateLimitService: defines inner closure runRedis(operationName, operationPromise) → wraps with withTimeout(200ms)
[Redis FAIL] runRedis → {ok:false} → RateLimitService → #checkAndIncrementInDb(dbKey, max, period, methodName) → RETURN
[Redis OK, windowStart=0] RateLimitService → Database: SELECT * FROM rl_usage_fixed_window WHERE key=?
[DB row found] RateLimitService → runRedis('window-start-seed', redisClient.set(windowStartKey, windowStart)) + runRedis('count-seed', redisClient.set(countKey, count))

[DB row NOT found, windowStart still 0] RateLimitService → runRedis('window-start-init', redisClient.set(windowStartKey, now)) + runRedis('count-init', redisClient.set(countKey, 0)) + Database: INSERT INTO rl_usage_fixed_window
[window expired: windowStart+period < now] RateLimitService → runRedis('window-start-reset', ...) + runRedis('count-reset', ...) + Database: UPDATE SET window_start=now, count=0
[Redis FAIL on count-read] RateLimitService → #checkAndIncrementInDb(dbKey, max, period, methodName) → RETURN
[current >= max] RateLimitService → APIError.create('rate_limit_exceeded') → THROW (request DENIED, HTTP 429)
[current < max] RateLimitService → runRedis('count-incr', redisClient.incr(countKey)) + Database: UPDATE SET count=count+1 WHERE key=? (request ALLOWED)
fallback_flow: [row exists] use DB windowStart and count

key_methods.RateLimitService.check_and_increment: async (key: string, max: number, period: number, options={global?:boolean}) → void | throws APIError
key_methods.RateLimitService.#checkAndIncrementInDb: async ({dbKey, max, period, methodName}) → void | throws APIError
key_methods.runRedis (closure): async (operationName: string, operationPromise: Promise) → {ok: boolean, value: any}
key_methods.withTimeout: async (operationPromise, timeoutMs=200, timeoutMessage) → value | throws timeout Error

key_relationships: RateLimitService uses SyncFeature([check_and_increment]) — serializes concurrent calls
RateLimitService reads Context to identify consumer/user
DriverService wraps call in svc_su.sudo(policy_holder, ...) to enforce policy holder identity
ServiceManager registers/initializes RateLimitService (register → maybe_init_ → init_service_ → Running)
important_notes: Fixed-window counter strategy: window resets when windowStart+period < now
Redis is the PRIMARY store; DB (rl_usage_fixed_window) is fallback and persistent sync
Redis key scope: global option uses rateLimitKey alone; per-server uses `${server_id}:${rateLimitKey}`
SyncFeature ensures check_and_increment is not concurrently re-entrant for same service instance
APIError 'rate_limit_exceeded' carries method_name and {max, period} metadata
EdgeRateLimitService (login.js) is a SEPARATE service for edge-level rate limiting (not part of this flow)
ServiceManager.get() returns undefined if service not yet Running; use aget() for async await
